nav.backfill_sub: Pull older history into the queue
fab.browse_chats_sub: Pick a chat to monitor or backfill
sidebar.signout.confirm_body: You'll need to log in again on the next visit. Telegram accounts and downloads stay put.
settings.video_player.title: Video Player
settings.video_player.autoplay_help: Start playing automatically when a video opens.
settings.video_player.start_muted_help: Required by most browsers for autoplay to work. Click 🔊 in the player to unmute.
settings.video_player.loop_help: Restart from the beginning when the clip ends.
settings.video_player.auto_advance_help: When a video ends, open the next file in the gallery automatically.
settings.video_player.resume_help: Resume each clip where you left off the next time you open it.
settings.video_player.show_pip_help: Hide if you never use PiP — frees space in the toolbar.
settings.video_player.show_speed_help: Hide to clean up the toolbar; you can still set the default speed below.
settings.video_player.dbl_tap_fs_help: Mobile-friendly shortcut. Single tap still toggles play/pause.
settings.notifications_help: Get a system toast when downloads finish.
settings.security.force_https_help: Redirect HTTP requests to HTTPS. Sends an HSTS header on every secure response. Localhost is exempt. Set TRUST_PROXY=1 if running behind a reverse proxy.
settings.security.force_https_confirm_bo: Every HTTP request will 308-redirect to HTTPS. Make sure your reverse proxy has a working TLS cert and TRUST_PROXY=1 is set, otherwise the dashboard becomes unreachable. Localhost is exempt so you can still recover from the host.
settings.security.rate_limit_help: Throttles /api/* per IP (default off). Enable only if exposing the dashboard publicly. Login + setup limiters stay on regardless.
settings.security.guest_password_help: Guests can browse and watch media but cannot delete files, change settings, or manage Telegram accounts.
settings.security.guest_enabled_on: Enabled — share the guest password with read-only viewers.
settings.security.guest_enabled_off: Disabled — set a guest password and toggle on to share view-only access.
settings.security.guest_enabled_toast_of: Guest access disabled — existing guest sessions revoked
settings.security.guest_clear_body: This will disable guest access and sign out anyone currently logged in as guest.
settings.security.guest_password_short: Guest password must be at least 8 characters
settings.security.guest_same_as_admin: Guest password must differ from admin
errors.admin_only: Admin only — sign in as admin to perform this action.
settings.federation.subtitle: Settings that span every paired peer in your cluster. Pair peers and manage tokens under Maintenance → Cluster.
settings.federation.empty_hint: No peers paired yet. Replication settings below take effect once at least one peer is paired.
settings.federation.replicate.title: Replication policy
settings.federation.replicate.help: Pick which config keys mirror across the cluster. Local stays on this peer. Cluster mirrors edits to every paired peer (last-writer-wins). Cluster-excl mirrors but lets receivers override locally.
settings.federation.failover.title: Failover grace window
settings.federation.failover.help: Minutes the backup peer waits after the owner goes silent before taking over downloading. Lower = faster recovery, higher = fewer false failovers from a slow peer.
settings.accounts.session_help: Sessions are stored AES-256 encrypted under data/sessions/.
settings.accounts.empty_html: No Telegram accounts yet. Click Add account above to get started.
settings.tg_api.hash_placeholder: Stored locally; never sent to the dashboard once saved
settings.tg_api.hash_keep_help: Leave blank to keep the existing hash.
settings.rate.title: Rate Limits
settings.rate.polling_help: How often to check for new messages.
settings.size.title: Size Limits
settings.size.help: Files exceeding these limits will be skipped.
settings.disk.cap_help: Use units like "10 GB", "500 MB", or "1 TB". Leave the dropdown empty for no cap.
settings.disk.rotate_help: When the cap is exceeded, delete the oldest downloads first. Pinned items are kept.
settings.proxy.title: Proxy
settings.privacy.title: Privacy
settings.privacy.allow_dm_help: Lets the Groups picker show 1-on-1 chats. Off by default — once enabled you'll see DMs in the dialog list and can opt them in individually.
settings.preset.title: Quick Presets
settings.advanced.title: Advanced
settings.advanced.subtitle: Hot-path tunables. Defaults match the original hardcoded values — only change these if you know what you are doing.
settings.advanced.collapsed_hint: Click to expand the advanced tunables section.
settings.maintenance_link.title: Maintenance
settings.maintenance_link.body: Run the catalogue / thumbnail / NSFW / cluster tools — open the dedicated Maintenance hub.
settings.advanced.min_concurrency_help: Auto-scaler floor. FloodWait throttles snap back to this.
settings.advanced.max_concurrency_help: Auto-scaler ceiling. Higher risks Telegram FLOOD_WAIT bans.
settings.advanced.scaler_sec_help: How often to add/remove workers based on queue depth.
settings.advanced.idle_sleep_help: Worker pause when no job is queued. Lower = snappier.
settings.advanced.spillover_help: When the history queue exceeds this size, new jobs spill to disk to bound RAM. Realtime never spills.
settings.advanced.backpressure_help: Pause iteration when downloader queue exceeds this size.
settings.advanced.backpressure_wait_help: Abort backfill if queue stays full this long.
settings.advanced.long_break_help: 60-120s coffee break. 0 disables. Helps avoid bans.
settings.advanced.integrity_min_help: How often to walk every DB row and prune dead entries.
settings.advanced.integrity_batch_help: Concurrent stat() calls. Bigger = faster, more FD pressure.
settings.advanced.session_days_help: New login cookies expire after this many days. Existing sessions keep their original expiry.
settings.danger.title: Danger Zone
settings.danger.help: These actions are irreversible. All files, database records, and group configurations will be permanently deleted.
settings.page.title: Settings
settings.page.subtitle: System Configuration
groups.empty.title: No groups yet
groups.empty.body: Add a Telegram chat from the Chats page to start downloading.
groups.page.title: Manage Groups
groups.page.subtitle: Configure monitoring and filters
groups.no_account.title: No Telegram account yet
groups.no_account.body: Add your Telegram account to load chats and start downloading.
group.modal.title: Group Settings
group.fwd.title: Auto Forward
group.fwd.subtitle: Forward media to another channel
group.fwd.dest_help: Leave empty to use "Telegram Downloader Storage".
group.fwd.delete_after_help: Remove local file after successful upload.
group.accounts.title: Account Assignment
group.accounts.subtitle: Choose which Telegram account handles each role for this group
group.accounts.monitor_help: This account will watch and download media from this group.
group.accounts.forward_help: This account will re-upload forwarded media (Auto Forward).
group.accounts.manage_hint: Manage accounts under Settings → Telegram Accounts.
group.cluster.title: Cluster routing
group.cluster.subtitle: Route this group's downloads to a specific peer. Other peers see the catalogue via the bridge but stay silent on Telegram.
group.cluster.owner_help: Designates which peer downloads this group. Empty = any peer with the matching account.
group.cluster.backup_help: If the owner is silent past the cluster grace window, the backup takes over downloading.
group.topics.title: Forum-Topic Filter
group.topics.help_html: If this group is a forum (supergroup with topics), restrict downloads to specific topic IDs. Comma-separated. Get a topic ID from a Telegram message link like https://t.me/c/<id>/<topicId>/<msg>.
group.topics.note: Empty list = monitor every topic. Toggle off to disable filtering entirely.
group.backfill.title: Backfill History
group.backfill.help: Pull older messages from this group into the downloader.
group.backfill.all_warn: Pulling all history may download tens of thousands of files and take hours.
group.backfill.all_confirm: Backfill ALL history for this chat? This may take hours and download a lot of data.
group.backfill.confirm_n: Download the last {n} messages of "{name}" into the queue?
group.backfill.queued: Job {id} queued — watch the Engine card for progress.
backfill.page.title: Backfill
backfill.page.subtitle: Pull older messages from a chat into the queue
backfill.empty.title: No active backfills
backfill.empty.body: Start one below to pull older messages from a chat.
backfill.active.title: Active backfills
backfill.recent.title: Recent backfills (30 days)
backfill.recent.clear_body: This removes every entry from the Recent backfills list. Running jobs are preserved. Files already downloaded are not affected.
backfill.start.title: Start a new backfill
backfill.start.group_placeholder: Type to search your monitored chats…
backfill.start.no_results: No matching chats. Add one from the Chats page first.
backfill.start.warn_all: Pulling all history may download tens of thousands of files and take hours.
backfill.row.attempts_help: Newest attempt shown — older attempts collapsed.
backfill.modal.title: Backfill History
backfill.modal.help: Open the Backfill page with this chat preselected.
backfill.modal.deeplink_hint: Opens the Backfill page so you can review running and recent jobs in one place.
viewer.empty.group_title: No downloaded media for this group yet
viewer.empty.group_body_admin: Either nothing has been downloaded yet or the catalogue is out of sync. Run a Backfill to pull older messages, double-check the media filters in Group Settings, or re-index from disk if files exist on disk but not in the database.
viewer.empty.group_body_guest: Either nothing has been downloaded yet or the catalogue is empty for this chat. Ask an admin to run a Backfill.
viewer.empty.all_body_admin: Nothing has been downloaded yet. Add a Telegram account and enable a chat under Groups, or paste a t.me/ link to download a single message.
viewer.empty.all_body_guest: Nothing has been downloaded yet. Ask an admin to add a Telegram account and enable some chats.
viewer.all_media.title: All Media
viewer.all_media.subtitle: All downloaded files
viewer.bulk.title: Delete selected files?
viewer.bulk.confirm: Delete {count} file(s)? This cannot be undone.
viewer.delete.title: Delete file?
gallery.scope.label: Scope
viewer.video.share_title: Create a share link — friends can open the file without logging in
viewer.text.title: Plain text
viewer.code.title: Code
viewer.markdown.title: Markdown
viewer.fallback.title: No inline preview
viewer.fallback.message: This file type doesn't have a built-in preview.
viewer.fallback.office_message: Office documents render best in their native app — download to open.
maintenance.dedup.already_running: A dedup scan is already running on another client.
onboard.configure_api.title: Step 1 of 3 — paste your Telegram API credentials
onboard.configure_api.body_html: Get apiId + apiHash from my.telegram.org, then save them under Settings → Telegram API.
onboard.add_account.title: Step 2 of 3 — add a Telegram account
onboard.add_account.body_html: Sign in with your phone number (and 2FA if you have it). Sessions are stored encrypted under data/sessions/.
onboard.enable_group.title: Step 3 of 3 — pick a chat to monitor
onboard.enable_group.body_html: Open the Groups page, click a chat to add it, or paste a t.me/... link from the top bar to download a single message right away.
picker.title: Pick a destination
picker.no_account: Add a Telegram account first to pick a forward destination.
link.title: Download from Telegram Link
link.help_html: One URL per line. Supports t.me/<chan>/<msg>, /c/<id>/<msg>, forum-topic links and tg://.
link.warn_empty: Paste at least one Telegram link.
stories.title: Download Stories
stories.help: Pull active Stories from any username your account can see.
stories.none_visible: No active stories visible to your account.
account.remove.confirm: Remove account "{id}"? The encrypted session file will be deleted.
purge.group.title: Purge group data?
purge.group.confirm:
Delete all data for "{name}"?

Files, database records, and configuration will be permanently removed.
purge.all.title: Purge ALL data?
purge.all.confirm1:
Delete ALL data?

All files, database records, group configurations, and photos will be permanently removed.
purge.all.confirm2: Are you sure? This cannot be undone.
toast.proxy_saved: Proxy saved — restart the monitor for it to take effect.
toast.proxy_reachable: ✓ Reachable ({ms}ms TCP) — protocol handshake happens at monitor start.
toast.password_short: New password must be at least 8 characters.
shortcuts.title: Keyboard shortcuts
shortcuts.tip: Tip — none of these fire while you're typing in a text field.
update.click_for_release: {version} is out — click to view release notes.
ws.giveup: Lost connection to server — click WS dot to retry.
maintenance.logs.timeout: Log read timed out (file too large or server busy).
maintenance.title: Maintenance
maintenance.subtitle: Operational tasks that used to need a CLI session. Everything below is safe to run from the browser.
maintenance.resync.title: Resync Telegram Dialogs
maintenance.resync.help: Re-resolve every group's name + profile photo against Telegram. Use when an "Unknown chat" lingers in the list.
maintenance.restart.title: Restart Monitor
maintenance.restart.help: Stops and re-starts the realtime monitor in-process. Picks up settings / proxy / account changes without bouncing the container.
maintenance.restart.confirm: Stop and restart the realtime monitor? In-flight downloads will be paused briefly.
maintenance.db_check.title: Database Integrity Check
maintenance.db_check.help: Runs PRAGMA integrity_check against the SQLite catalogue. Read-only.
maintenance.db_vacuum.title: VACUUM Database
maintenance.db_vacuum.help: Reclaims disk space after lots of deletions. Briefly locks the DB — avoid during a heavy backfill.
maintenance.db_vacuum.confirm: Run VACUUM on the SQLite database? It briefly locks the DB and may take a minute on large datasets.
maintenance.verify.title: Verify Files on Disk
maintenance.verify.help: Drop catalogue rows whose file went missing on disk. Runs hourly in the background; this button forces it now.
maintenance.dedup.title: Find Duplicate Files
maintenance.dedup.help: Hash every file and group byte-identical copies. You pick which to keep before anything is deleted.
maintenance.dedup.starting: Scanning files — this may take a while on the first run.
maintenance.dedup.summary: {sets} duplicate sets · {dupes} extra copies · up to {freed} reclaimable.
maintenance.dedup.help_pick: Each set below contains byte-identical files. Tick the copies you want to delete; the rest are kept. Default selection deletes everything except the oldest copy.
maintenance.dedup.confirm_body: Permanently delete {n} file(s) from disk and database?
maintenance.thumbs.title: Build Thumbnails for Older Files
maintenance.thumbs.help: Generates compact WebP previews for every file in the library. New downloads are thumbnailed automatically; this sweep covers anything that landed before the feature shipped.
maintenance.thumbs.rebuild_body: Wipes every cached thumbnail. The next gallery scroll regenerates them on demand. Useful when previews look stale or after a quality tweak.
maintenance.nsfw.title: Scan Images for NSFW (18+)
maintenance.nsfw.help: Scans every photo in the library and flags ones the classifier thinks are NOT 18+ — likely false matches that need to be purged. Runs locally (no upload). The model downloads once on first use (~80 MB).
maintenance.nsfw.scan_tooltip: Run the NSFW classifier across every photo in the library. Local-only, no upload.
maintenance.nsfw.disabled: Disabled. Enable in Settings → Advanced → NSFW review tool.
maintenance.nsfw.started: Scan started — will notify when done
maintenance.nsfw.empty: No candidates — the library is clean.
maintenance.nsfw.review_help: These photos scored as NOT 18+ (below the threshold). Tick the ones you want to delete; a confirmation sheet appears before anything is removed. If a row IS 18+ but the classifier got it wrong, click "Mark as 18+" to keep it and skip future scans.
maintenance.nsfw.mark_keep_title: Mark as 18+ — keep and skip future scans
maintenance.nsfw.confirm_body: Permanently delete {n} photo(s) from disk and database?
maintenance.nsfw.done_clean: NSFW scan done — library is clean.
settings.advanced.nsfw_help: Off by default. Enable to surface a Maintenance button that scans every photo, classifies 18+ vs not-18+, and lists the not-18+ rows for admin review + delete. Runs entirely on this server (no external service); the model downloads once on first scan.
settings.advanced.nsfw_enable_help: Adds the "Scan images for NSFW" button to Maintenance.
settings.advanced.nsfw_threshold_help: Score >= this counts as 18+ (kept). Below = surfaced for review. Higher = stricter.
settings.advanced.nsfw_concurrency_help: Parallel inferences. Higher uses more CPU/RAM during scan; defaults to 1.
settings.advanced.auto_first_backfill_he: When you add a brand-new group, automatically pull the most recent N messages so the gallery isn't empty. Disable to keep the manual flow.
settings.advanced.auto_first_limit_help: Messages to pull on first add. 0 disables.
settings.advanced.auto_catchup_help: After a container restart, auto-spawn a backfill for the gap between the last stored message and Telegram's current top.
settings.advanced.batch_insert_help: DB rows buffered before commit during backfill. Higher = faster on small files; uses a tiny bit more RAM.
backfill.already_running: A backfill is already running for this group
jobs.already_running: Already running on another tab — waiting for it to finish.
maintenance.update.title: Install Update
maintenance.update.help: Pulls the latest container image and recreates the container in place. The data volume and config are preserved; the SQLite database is snapshotted to data/backups/ first. The dashboard reconnects automatically once the new container is healthy.
maintenance.update.no_watchtower: enable the auto-update profile to use this
update.click_to_install: v{version} is out — click for install / release notes
update.sheet_help: Installing pulls the new image and recreates this container. Your data volume and config are preserved; the SQLite database is snapshotted to data/backups/ first. The dashboard reconnects automatically once the new container passes its healthcheck.
update.confirm_body: Your downloads, sessions, config, and SQLite database are preserved — the data volume is mounted independently of the container, and the database is snapshotted to data/backups/ before the swap. The dashboard will go offline briefly while the new image swaps in (~30 seconds), then reconnect automatically.
update.overlay_body: Pulling the new image and restarting the container. The dashboard will reconnect automatically once the new version passes its healthcheck.
update.no_watchtower: Watchtower sidecar is not configured. See docker-compose.yml comments to enable the auto-update profile.
update.help_not_docker: You are running outside Docker — pull the latest source and restart manually.
update.help_no_watchtower_html: To enable: docker compose --profile auto-update up -d after setting WATCHTOWER_HTTP_API_TOKEN in .env. See docker-compose.yml for the full setup.
update.stalled_body: The new container has not come online after 2 minutes. Watchtower may still be pulling a slow image, or the new container may be failing its healthcheck. Check `docker logs <watchtower>` and `docker logs <main-container>` for clues.
update.error.WATCHTOWER_UNREACHABLE: Watchtower sidecar is unreachable. Check that the container is running with the `auto-update` profile enabled.
update.error.WATCHTOWER_UNAUTHENTICATED: Watchtower rejected the API token. Re-generate WATCHTOWER_HTTP_API_TOKEN in .env and restart with `docker compose --profile auto-update up -d`.
update.error.DB_CORRUPT: The live SQLite database failed its integrity check. Run Maintenance → DB integrity and recover before retrying.
update.error.BACKUP_FAILED: Pre-update database snapshot failed. Check disk space and write permissions on data/backups/.
update.error.BACKUP_VERIFY_FAILED: The freshly-written snapshot is unreadable or torn. The bad file has been removed; check disk health before retrying.
update.error.TRIGGER_FAILED: Watchtower returned a non-2xx response when the swap was triggered. Check `docker logs <watchtower>` for details.
update.error.AUTO_UPDATE_UNAVAILABLE: Auto-update is not available in this deployment. Enable it via `docker compose --profile auto-update up -d`.
update.error.unknown: Update failed for an unknown reason. See server logs for details.
update.history.title: Update history
update.history.help: Audit log of every Install update click — the structured error code makes repeat failures easy to diagnose.
update.history.empty: No updates have been triggered from this dashboard yet.
maintenance.hub.updates.title: Updates
maintenance.hub.updates.body: Install the latest release in one click and audit every past attempt — what failed, when, and why.
maintenance.shares.title: Active Share Links
maintenance.shares.help: View, search, and revoke every share link issued from this dashboard.
share.title: Share This File
share.help: Anyone with the link can open this file without logging in. The link expires automatically and can be revoked at any time.
share.label: Note (optional)
share.copy_failed: Could not copy — select the link manually
share.revoke_body: Anyone holding the link will immediately stop being able to open the file. This cannot be undone — you can issue a new link if you change your mind.
share.error.load: Could not open Share — try again
maintenance.logs.title: Log Files
maintenance.logs.help: View or download the tail of any logfile under data/logs/ — handy for filing bug reports without SSHing into the container.
maintenance.config.title: View Raw config.json
maintenance.config.help: Open the on-disk config (with secrets redacted) in a viewer sheet. For audit / sanity-check.
maintenance.config.redacted_help: Sensitive fields (apiHash, password hash, proxy password) are redacted.
maintenance.export.title: Export Telegram Session
maintenance.export.help: Decrypts a saved data/sessions/<id>.enc and shows the gramJS string so you can migrate the account to another instance. Sensitive — anyone with this string can act as the account.
maintenance.export.warn: Anyone with this string can act as the account. Treat it like a password.
maintenance.signout_all.title: Sign Out Everywhere
maintenance.signout_all.help: Revokes every dashboard session token. You'll be sent back to the login page. Use this after rotating the password or if you suspect a compromise.
maintenance.signout_all.confirm: Sign out every browser? You will be redirected to the login page.
maintenance.signout_all.password_prompt: Enter your dashboard password to sign out every browser:
maintenance.export.password_prompt: Enter your dashboard password to export the session string:
maintenance.export.auto_clear_help: The string auto-clears from the screen after 60 seconds. Copy it somewhere safe before then.
maintenance.hub.title: Maintenance
maintenance.hub.subtitle: Catalogue, thumbnails, NSFW review, logs, backup destinations — and AI search when enabled.
maintenance.hub.duplicates.title: Find duplicate files
maintenance.hub.duplicates.body: Hash every file and reclaim space from byte-identical copies.
maintenance.hub.thumbs.title: Build thumbnails
maintenance.hub.thumbs.body: Generate WebP previews for every catalogued file.
maintenance.hub.video.title: Optimise videos for streaming
maintenance.hub.video.body: Rewrite MP4s with `+faststart` so the player can seek + play audio without buffering the whole file.
maintenance.hub.nsfw.title: NSFW review
maintenance.hub.nsfw.body: Five-tier classifier — keep what is confidently 18+, delete what is not.
maintenance.hub.ai.title: AI face clustering
maintenance.hub.ai.body: Face clustering via a local Python sidecar (insightface buffalo_l) — opt-in, no upload.
maintenance.ai.subtitle: Face clustering — recognise people across your library using a local Python service.
maintenance.ai.faces.title: Face clustering
maintenance.ai.faces.desc: Detects faces with insightface buffalo_l, groups recurring people into clusters via DBSCAN.
maintenance.ai.faces.providers.probe_cap: Detect which GPU/CPU backend this host can run the face model on. CUDA → CoreML → DirectML → OpenVINO → CPU, in order of speed.
maintenance.ai.faces.threshold_help: Calibrated on real 900+ photo data. 0.8–1.0 = strict (precise). 1.05 = peak (recommended). 1.10+ starts merging different people; 1.15+ collapses everyone into one mega-cluster.
maintenance.ai.faces.model_help: Switching the model requires a Re-cluster — embedding spaces differ across presets.
maintenance.ai.reindex_confirm_body: This wipes EVERY face detection and EVERY person cluster, then re-scans every photo. Existing labels survive only if matching faces are detected again.
maintenance.ai.reindex_kicked: Reindex started — every photo will be re-detected.
maintenance.ai.reindex_tooltip: Wipes every face detection and every person cluster, then re-scans every photo. Use after switching the detector model.
maintenance.ai.scan_tooltip: Detect faces on every photo missing an index pass, then re-cluster recurring people.
maintenance.ai.master_toggle_help: Master switch. When off, no scans run, the sidecar stops accepting work, and auto-index on new downloads is skipped.
maintenance.ai.auto_toggle_help: When on, every newly-downloaded photo is queued for face detection in the background. Turn off to scan only via the buttons above.
maintenance.ai.install.title: Install GPU acceleration
maintenance.ai.install.desc: One click installs the matching onnxruntime backend for this host (DirectML on Windows, CUDA on Linux + NVIDIA, OpenVINO on Linux + Intel, CoreML on macOS, CPU elsewhere) and starts the sidecar. Takes 1–5 minutes the first time while wheels download.
maintenance.ai.install.running: Installing… (this can take a few minutes on first run)
maintenance.ai.faces.providers.label: Inference provider
maintenance.ai.faces.providers.help: Auto is the safe default; pick a specific backend only after a probe shows it works. Falls back to CPU if the chosen backend isn't available.
maintenance.ai.faces.providers.probe_lab: Test which inference provider this host can use
maintenance.ai.faces.providers.none: No working provider — falling back to CPU
maintenance.ai.coming_soon.title: Coming soon
maintenance.ai.coming_soon.list: Object detection · OCR · Face quality ranking · Smart albums
maintenance.ai.disabled_pill_help: Capability is disabled. Toggle it on to run scans.
maintenance.ai.people.heading: People
maintenance.ai.people_empty: No people clustered yet. Run a scan above.
maintenance.ai.delete_confirm: Delete this cluster? Faces will become unassigned.
maintenance.ai.merge_no_other: No other clusters to merge with.
maintenance.ai.merge_prompt: Type the cluster id of the target — every face in this cluster moves there.
maintenance.ai.merge_confirm: This cluster will be deleted and its faces will move to the target cluster. Cannot be undone.
maintenance.ai.split_prompt: Comma-separated face ids to move into a new cluster. Find them in the photos grid (inspect element → data-face-id).
maintenance.ai.search_disabled: AI search is disabled — enable it in Maintenance → AI.
maintenance.hub.logs.title: Log viewer
maintenance.hub.logs.body: Realtime tail of every backend log source — no docker logs needed.
maintenance.hub.backup.title: Backup destinations
maintenance.hub.backup.body: NAS / S3 / SFTP / Google Drive / Dropbox mirror + scheduled snapshots.
maintenance.hub.cluster.title: Cluster mode
maintenance.hub.cluster.body: Pair multiple instances so they federate downloads, gallery, and dedup. Each peer keeps its own data; the dashboard sees them as one library.
maintenance.cluster.subtitle: Federate multiple instances. Files, downloads, and dedup span every paired peer.
settings.advanced.thumbs.hwaccel_help: Off is the safe default. Pick the matching backend if the host has the matching driver and (in Docker) /dev/dri is passed through. Falls back to CPU silently if the chosen backend isn't available.
settings.advanced.thumbs.hwaccel.vaapi: VAAPI — Intel iGPU / AMD on Linux
settings.advanced.thumbs.hwaccel_probe_l: Test what this server can use
settings.advanced.thumbs.hwaccel_probe_h: Click to ask the running ffmpeg which acceleration backends are actually usable on this host. Backends shown are both compiled into ffmpeg AND reachable (driver loaded, /dev/dri accessible in Docker, etc.). Pick a matching value in the dropdown to use it; leave Off if nothing shows up.
settings.advanced.thumbs.hwaccel_probe_n: No hardware backends — this host is CPU-only.
group.rescue.title: Rescue Mode
group.rescue.help: Only keeps messages that get deleted from this chat within X hours. Anything still on Telegram after that gets pruned locally.
settings.rescue.title: Rescue Mode
settings.rescue.help: When enabled by default, every monitored group keeps only messages that get deleted from Telegram within the retention window. Per-group override available in Group Settings.
settings.rescue.default: Default to Rescue Mode for New Groups
viewer.badge.pending_tooltip: Will be auto-deleted at {time} unless source is deleted.
toast.rescue_default_on: Rescue mode default on — save to apply.
toast.rescue_default_off: Rescue mode default off — save to apply.
queue.page.title: Queue
queue.page.subtitle: Active + pending + recently finished downloads
queue.confirm.cancel_n: Stop {{n}} downloads? Any partial bytes will be discarded.
queue.empty.title: Queue is empty
queue.empty.body: Start the engine or kick off a backfill — every download flows through here.
queue.throttle.label: Max Speed
queue.duplicate.tooltip: Same file already exists on disk — no new bytes written, this row points at the existing copy.
share.maint.empty_filter: No links match the current filter.
share.maint.cleanup_help: Revoke every link that already expired — keeps the list tidy.
share.maint.cleanup_confirm_body: Permanently revoke {n} expired link(s)? Anyone holding them already can't open the file — this just removes them from the list.
queue.confirm.cancel_all: Cancel every queued download? Active jobs continue.
queue.confirm.cancel: Stop this download? Any partial bytes will be discarded.
maintenance.seekbar.subtitle: Generate WebP sprite sheets for video hover-preview thumbnails.
maintenance.seekbar.enable_help: Generates WebP sprite sheets so the video player can paint a hover-preview frame on the seek bar. Off by default.
maintenance.seekbar.auto_help: When a new video lands, queue it for sprite generation immediately. Realtime jumps ahead of any backfill.
maintenance.seekbar.wipe_confirm_body: {count} sprites · {size} on disk will be removed.
maintenance.seekbar.kpi.title: Cache footprint
maintenance.seekbar.settings.title: Sprite settings
maintenance.seekbar.hwaccel.label: Hardware acceleration
maintenance.seekbar.hwaccel.help: auto picks the best backend per platform (cuda/vaapi/qsv on Linux x86; videotoolbox on macOS; cuda/qsv/d3d11va on Windows; v4l2m2m on Pi). Falls back to CPU silently.
maintenance.seekbar.sidecar.binary_missi: sidecar binary missing — using ffmpeg fallback
maintenance.seekbar.health.title: System health
maintenance.seekbar.settings.desc: Defaults are tuned for hover scrubbing on Telegram clips; tweak only if your library has very long files or a tight disk budget.
maintenance.seekbar.settings.concurrency: How many sprite encodes the Go sidecar runs in parallel. 4 is the sweet spot on 4–8-core hosts.
maintenance.seekbar.hwaccel.backend_help: Auto is safe; pick a specific backend only after the probe shows it works. Falls back to CPU silently if the chosen backend can't initialise.
maintenance.hub.seekbar.title: Seekbar previews
maintenance.hub.seekbar.body: WebP sprite-sheet hover previews on the video player seek bar. Auto-spawns a Go sidecar; falls back to in-process ffmpeg.
maintenance.duplicates.title: Find duplicate files
maintenance.duplicates.subtitle: Hash every file and reclaim space from byte-identical copies. The first run is slow on large libraries; subsequent runs only hash newly-downloaded files.
maintenance.duplicates.bulk.heading: Apply to all sets
maintenance.duplicates.empty: No scan yet — click "Scan" to begin.
maintenance.duplicates.scan.tooltip: Hash every catalogued file (SHA-256) and group byte-identical copies. Cheap on subsequent runs.
maintenance.duplicates.reindex.tooltip: Walk data/downloads/ and add catalogue rows for files that aren't tracked yet. Run after restoring from backup.
maintenance.duplicates.verify.tooltip: Walk every catalogue row and drop entries whose file is missing on disk. Same sweep that runs hourly in the background.
maintenance.duplicates.stats.last_scan_r: Last scan: {sets} duplicate set(s) · {extras} extra copies · {reclaim} reclaimable (scanned {scanned} files)
maintenance.thumbs.subtitle: Generate WebP previews for older files in the library. New downloads are thumbnailed automatically; this sweep covers anything that landed before the feature shipped.
maintenance.thumbs.build_tooltip: Walks every catalogued file and generates a WebP thumb where one is missing.
maintenance.thumbs.wipe_tooltip: Wipes every cached thumbnail (including any legacy non-standard widths left over from earlier builds); the next gallery scroll regenerates them on demand at the standard width.
maintenance.thumbs.no_ffmpeg_chip: Video / audio thumbs disabled — ffmpeg missing on PATH
maintenance.thumbs.hwaccel_help: Off is the safe default. Pick the matching backend if the host has the matching driver + Docker has device passthrough (`/dev/dri` for VAAPI/QSV). Falls back to CPU silently if the chosen backend isn't available.
maintenance.thumbs.hwaccel.vaapi: VAAPI — Intel iGPU / AMD on Linux
maintenance.thumbs.hwaccel_probe_label: Test what this server can use
maintenance.thumbs.hwaccel_probe_none: No hardware backends available — CPU only
maintenance.thumbs.hwaccel_probe_help: Click to ask the running ffmpeg which acceleration backends are actually usable on this host. Backends shown are both compiled into ffmpeg AND reachable. Pick a matching value above; leave Off if nothing shows up.
maintenance.thumbs.warn_misses_help: When on, emits one consolidated "X thumb misses" warning every 30 minutes if the rate is high. Turn off if you find the warnings noisy and you've already verified your library.
maintenance.thumbs.gallery.title: Thumbnail preview
maintenance.thumbs.gallery.help: Newest first · scroll to load more · tiles render only when visible.
maintenance.thumbs.gallery.empty: No thumbnails to show yet — run Build all or wait for new downloads.
maintenance.thumbs.build_kind_tooltip: Build for one media class only
maintenance.thumbs.rebuild_body_n: Wipes {count} cached thumbnails ({size}). The next gallery scroll regenerates them on demand.
maintenance.video.subtitle: MP4 files whose `moov` index is at the end of the file break HTML5 seek and look like they have no audio. This sweep rewrites them in-place with `+faststart` so the player gets the index up front. Stream-copy, no re-encode.
maintenance.video.scan_tooltip: Backfill the moov rewrite across every MP4 / MOV / M4V already in the library. New downloads are handled automatically by the hook above.
maintenance.video.last_run_result: Last run: {optimized} optimised · {already} already faststart · {skipped} skipped (scanned {scanned})
maintenance.video.no_ffmpeg_chip: Faststart unavailable — ffmpeg missing on PATH
maintenance.video.stats_help: Library-wide totals. Pending = MP4s whose moov atom is still at the end of the file (eligible for the backfill action above). Skipped = files in containers that don't use moov (WebM, MKV) or that aren't on disk. Auto-optimise stats for new downloads are tracked separately at the top.
maintenance.video.auto.title: Auto-optimise on download
maintenance.video.auto.subtitle: Every newly-downloaded MP4 / MOV / M4V is moov-rewritten in the background before it lands in the gallery. No operator click required — these counters track the auto path only.
maintenance.video.auto.help: Counters persist across restarts. "Seen" = how many post-download decisions the hook handled. Use the backfill action below for files that were already in the library before auto-optimise existed.
maintenance.nsfw.subtitle: Five-tier classifier review — keep what's confidently 18+, delete what's confidently not, eyeball the borderline cases.
maintenance.nsfw.empty_db_hint: Library has no scannable photos — try Maintenance → Re-index from disk first if files exist on disk but the catalogue is empty.
maintenance.nsfw.settings_help: Off by default. The model downloads once on first scan (or click "Preload" to fetch it now). Runs entirely on this server — no third-party service.
maintenance.nsfw.enable_help: Surfaces this Maintenance tool + scores newly-downloaded photos in the background.
maintenance.nsfw.preload_on_start_help: Download + warm the model in the background when the server boots, so the first scan starts instantly.
maintenance.nsfw.model_help: Any HuggingFace `owner/model` id that ships an `image-classification` ONNX pipeline. The library auto-detects which precision variant is available.
maintenance.nsfw.dtype_help: Falls back to whichever variant the model actually publishes if the chosen one is missing.
maintenance.nsfw.threshold_help: Score >= this counts as 18+ (kept). Below = surfaced for review. Higher = stricter.
maintenance.nsfw.concurrency_help: Parallel inferences per scan. Higher uses more CPU/RAM; default 1 keeps the server responsive.
maintenance.nsfw.preload.started: Preload started — progress in the realtime log.
maintenance.nsfw.preload.already_loading: A preload is already in progress.
maintenance.nsfw.cache_clear.confirm_bod: The classifier cache directory will be emptied. The next scan or preload will re-download the model.
maintenance.nsfw.model_status.ready_help: Weights cached on disk — scans start instantly.
maintenance.nsfw.model_status.error_help: Load failed. Check the realtime log for details, then try a different model id or precision.
maintenance.nsfw.bulk.title: Bulk actions for "{tier}":
maintenance.nsfw.bulk.confirm_delete_bod: Permanently deletes every file in this tier from disk and database. This cannot be undone.
maintenance.nsfw.bulk.confirm_whitelist_: Marks every file in this tier as confirmed 18+. They will be skipped on future scans.
maintenance.nsfw.bulk.confirm_reclassify: Clears the cached score so the next scan run picks them up again.
maintenance.nsfw.row.keep_done: Kept — will re-classify on next scan
maintenance.logs.subtitle: Realtime tail of every backend log source
maintenance.reindex.confirm: Walk every download directory and re-add catalogue rows for files that aren't tracked yet?
maintenance.reindex.done: Re-index done — added {added} rows from {scanned} files.
maintenance.backup.subtitle: Mirror new downloads to S3 / SFTP / local NAS storage. Optional client-side encryption + scheduled snapshots.
maintenance.backup.empty: No destinations yet — click "Add destination" to mirror your library off-host.
maintenance.backup.unlock_help: Re-enter the encryption passphrase you set for this destination. The key is held in memory only — server restart prompts again.
maintenance.backup.remove_body: Stop syncing to "{name}"? Existing remote files stay put — this only deletes the local pointer.
maintenance.backup.field.name: Display name
maintenance.backup.provider_locked: Provider can't be changed after creation — remove + re-add to switch.
maintenance.backup.mode.mirror_help: Every newly-downloaded file is queued for upload right away.
maintenance.backup.mode.snapshot_help: Periodic full archive of db.sqlite + config + sessions, retained N copies.
maintenance.backup.mode.manual_help: No auto-uploads — fires only when you click "Run now".
maintenance.backup.enc.label: Encrypt uploads (AES-256-GCM)
maintenance.backup.enc.help: Files are encrypted on this host before upload — the remote sees only ciphertext. The passphrase derives the key (PBKDF2 200k iters); we do NOT store it. Lose the passphrase = lose the data.
maintenance.backup.cron_required: Cron expression required for snapshot mode
maintenance.backup.edit_secret_hint: Saved secrets are not shown — leave blank to keep, fill in to replace.
maintenance.backup.help.gdrive.title: How do I get clientId / clientSecret / refreshToken?
maintenance.backup.help.gdrive.step1: Open Google Cloud Console → "New project" (any name).
maintenance.backup.help.gdrive.step2: APIs & Services → Library → search "Google Drive API" → click Enable.
maintenance.backup.help.gdrive.step3: APIs & Services → Credentials → Create Credentials → OAuth client ID → application type "Desktop app". Save the client ID + client secret into the fields above.
maintenance.backup.help.gdrive.step4: Open the OAuth Playground → gear icon → "Use your own OAuth credentials" → paste client ID + secret. Pick scope https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.file → Authorize → Exchange authorization code for tokens → copy the refresh_token into the field above.
maintenance.backup.help.dropbox.title: How do I get appKey / appSecret / refreshToken?
maintenance.backup.help.dropbox.step1: Open the Dropbox developer console → Create app → "Scoped access" → "App folder" (recommended) or "Full Dropbox" → name your app.
maintenance.backup.help.dropbox.step2: Permissions tab → enable files.content.write, files.content.read, account_info.read → click Submit.
maintenance.backup.help.dropbox.step3: Settings tab → copy App key + App secret into the fields above. Generate a refresh token via the OAuth flow documented at dropbox.com/developers/documentation/http/documentation#authorization, or run scripts/setup-dropbox.js for a guided CLI walkthrough.
favorites.no_id: This row has no DB id — cannot pin
favorites.surface_top_help: Pinned items appear above other files in every gallery view.
gallery.context.copy_failed: Could not copy — paste from the address bar
gallery.context.share_no_id: This row has no DB id — share not available
gallery.context.delete_confirm: Delete this file? This cannot be undone.
viewer.pip.unsupported: Picture-in-picture isn't supported in this browser.
viewer.selection.zip_no_ids: Selected files have no DB id — re-open the page and try again.
dragdrop.title: Drop a t.me link to start downloading
dragdrop.hint: URL works for messages, channels, posts, and stories.
dragdrop.no_url: No t.me link found in the dropped data
prefs.notifications.title: System notifications
prefs.notifications.help: Receive a desktop / phone notification when downloads finish. Click the notification to focus the dashboard.
prefs.notifications.unsupported: Your browser doesn't support system notifications.
prefs.notifications.denied: Permission denied — re-enable in your browser's site settings.
prefs.shortcuts.title: Keyboard shortcuts
groups.accent_color_help: Used as the sidebar row's left edge and the gallery's tile selection ring when this group is open.
groups.description_help: Free-form notes shown under the group name.
storage.breakdown.title: Storage breakdown
tour.step.sidebar: This is the sidebar — your downloaded chats and the All Media gallery live here.
tour.step.paste: Paste any t.me link here to download from a public channel or message.
tour.step.first_download: Once a download finishes you'll see it in the gallery.
tour.step.viewer: Click any tile to open the in-app viewer with PiP, fullscreen, and downloads.
tour.step.settings: Settings live here — accounts, theme, security, backup destinations.
changelog.viewer.title: Release notes
cluster.tab.title: Cluster
cluster.tab.subtitle: Federate multiple instances. Files, downloads, and dedup span every paired peer.
cluster.identity.title: This peer
cluster.identity.subtitle: Your local instance — visible to paired peers under this name.
cluster.identity.name: Display name
cluster.identity.token.help: Both sides of a pairing must hold the same token. Treat it like a password.
cluster.identity.token.rotate.confirm: Rotate the cluster token? Every paired peer must re-pair against the new value.
cluster.identity.token.rotated: Token rotated. Re-pair every peer with the new value.
cluster.identity.token.set.help: Paste the cluster token from any peer already in the cluster. This peer will replace its own token with that value so they match — required before pairing them.
cluster.pairing_code.title: Pairing code
cluster.pairing_code.help: Show this code to the operator on the other peer. Valid for 5 minutes — single use.
cluster.peers.title: Paired peers
cluster.peers.empty: No peers yet. Add one to begin federating.
cluster.peer.add.title: Pair a remote peer
cluster.peer.add.help: Open Settings → Cluster on the remote instance and copy its URL + cluster token here.
cluster.peer.stream_mode.proxy.help: Browser fetches files from this peer's URL only. Works behind any NAT/firewall.
cluster.peer.stream_mode.direct.help: Browser is redirected to the owner peer. Faster but requires the owner to be browser-reachable.
cluster.peer.revoke.confirm: Revoke {name}? Cached catalog will be cleared. The remote peer keeps its own data.
cluster.error.bad_url: URL must start with http:// or https://
cluster.error.unreachable: Could not reach the peer URL — check the address + network
cluster.error.token_invalid: Token rejected — both peers must hold the same cluster token
cluster.error.self: That URL points back to this same instance
cluster.error.clock_skew: Peer clocks differ by more than 60 s — sync system time
cluster.audit.title: Recent cluster events
cluster.sweep.title: Cross-peer dedup
cluster.sweep.subtitle: Finds files duplicated across peers (same hash + size). Resolve by picking which copy to keep.
cluster.sweep.keep_help: Click which copy to keep — every other copy is removed.
groups.owner_peer.help: If set, only that peer downloads this group. Others see its files via the cluster bridge.
maintenance.nsfw.bulk.confirm_restore_bo: Move {n} photo(s) back into the gallery and clear their review state?
maintenance.nsfw.empty.never_scanned: No NSFW scan run yet — click Scan to populate this list.
maintenance.nsfw.empty.tier_with_suggest: No photos in this tier — try a wider threshold or rescan.
reauth.title: Sign in again
reauth.body: Your session expired. Enter the dashboard password to continue.
settings.engine.title: Realtime monitor
maintenance.recovery.subtitle: Resolve, disable, or delete groups that no loaded account can access.
maintenance.recovery.empty.title: Nothing to clean up
maintenance.recovery.empty.body: Every monitored group has a working Telegram account attached.
maintenance.recovery.delete_confirm_body: Remove {n} group(s) from your monitor list. This only removes the config entry — downloaded files on disk are kept.
maintenance.recovery.synthetic_tip: Synthetic unknown:* id from a CLI reindex
maintenance.recovery.reason.index_miss: Folder not in any loaded account's dialogs.
maintenance.recovery.reason.empty_folder: Synthetic id has no folder name.
maintenance.recovery.reason.banned: Channel exists but the loaded account was banned / kicked.
maintenance.hub.recovery.title: Recovery cleanup
maintenance.hub.recovery.body: Resolve, disable, or delete groups that no loaded Telegram account can access — typically residue from npm run recover.
group.data.delete_files_confirm: Drop every download row + on-disk file for this group. Group config (filters, monitor, accounts) is kept so the next pass can re-download fresh.
group.data.wipe_all_confirm: Removes the group from your monitor list, drops every download row, and deletes the on-disk folder. This is destructive.
group.data.actions_help: "Delete files only" clears the gallery + on-disk files but keeps the group monitored — useful for re-downloading fresh. "Wipe all data" also removes the group from your monitor list.
